---
title: "Maeve"
author: "Maeve Gualtieri-Reed"
date: "2026-03-31"
output: pdf_document
---


```{r}
library(here)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(forecast)
library(cowplot)
library(writexl)
```



# Load in data set

```{r}
load_full <- read_excel("load.xlsx") %>% 
  mutate(daily = rowMeans(across(3:26), na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  mutate(date = ymd(date)) %>% 
  select(date,daily)

load_train <- load_full %>% 
  filter(date < as.Date("2010-07-01"))

temp_full <- read.csv("data/temp_processed.csv")
humidity_full <- read.csv("data/humidity_processed.csv")
```

# Create time series object

```{r}
ts_load_full <- msts(load_full$daily, 
                seasonal.periods =c(7,365.25), #7 = weekly, 365.25 = yearly 
                start=c(2005,1,1))

ts_load_train <- msts(load_train$daily, 
                seasonal.periods =c(7,365.25), #7 = weekly, 365.25 = yearly 
                start=c(2005,1,1))
```

# Examine data

```{r}
autoplot(ts_load_full)
autoplot(ts_load_train)
```

# Models

## ARIMA + fourier
### Model 1: k = 2,12; no exog
```{r}
ARIMA_Four_train <- auto.arima(ts_load_train, 
                             seasonal=FALSE, 
                             lambda=0,
                             xreg=fourier(ts_load_train, 
                                          K=c(2,12)),
                             approximation = TRUE
                             )

#Forecast with ARIMA fit
#also need to specify h for fourier terms
ARIMA_Four_train_forecast <- forecast(ARIMA_Four_fit,
                           xreg=fourier(ts_load_train,
                                        K=c(2,12),
                                        h=31),
                           h=31
                           ) 

#Plot foresting results
autoplot(ARIMA_Four_train_forecast) + ylab("Load")

#Plot model + observed data
autoplot(ts_load_full) +
  autolayer(ARIMA_Four_train_forecast, series="ARIMA_FOURIER",PI=FALSE) +
  ylab("Load")

#Train and forecast with full data set
ARIMA_Four_full <- auto.arima(ts_load_full, 
                             seasonal=FALSE, 
                             lambda=0,
                             xreg=fourier(ts_load_full, 
                                          K=c(2,12)),
                             approximation = TRUE
                             )

ARIMA_Four_full_forecast <- forecast(ARIMA_Four_fit,
                           xreg=fourier(ts_load_train,
                                        K=c(2,12),
                                        h=31),
                           h=31
                           ) 

ARIMA_four1<- data.frame(
  date = seq(as.Date("2011-07-01"), length.out = 31, by = "day"),
  value = ARIMA_Four_full_forecast$mean
)

write_csv(ARIMA_four1, 'Submissions/ARIMAfour1.csv')
```

### Model 2: k = 2,12; include temp and humidity
Chatgpt used to understand how to include temp and humidity along with fourier in xreg

```{r}
fourier_terms <- fourier(ts_load_full, K=c(2,12))
xreg_data <- left_join(temp_full, humidity_full, by = "date") %>% 
  head(-1) %>% 
  select(avg_temp, avg_humidity) %>% 
  cbind(fourier_terms)

write_csv(xreg_data,"data/xreg_data.csv")

#might need matrix? still not working

ARIMA_Four_full2 <- auto.arima(ts_load_full, 
                             seasonal=FALSE, 
                             lambda=0,
                             xreg=xred_data
                             )

ARIMA_Four_full_forecast2 <- forecast(ARIMA_Four_full2,
                           xreg=xreg_data,
                           h=31
                           ) 
```

## ETS
```{r}
#Fit and forecast STL + ETS model to data
ETS_train1 <-  stlf(ts_load_train,h=31)

#Plot foresting results
autoplot(ETS_train1) + ylab("Load")

#Plot model + observed data
autoplot(ts_load_full) +
  autolayer(ETS_train1, series="STL + ETS",PI=FALSE) +
  ylab("Load")

ETS_train_scores<- accuracy(ETS_train1$mean,ts_load_full)  

ETS_fit1<- stlf(ts_load_full,h=31)
ETS_output1<- data.frame(
  date = seq(as.Date("2011-07-01"), length.out = 31, by = "day"),
  value = ETS_fit1$mean
)

write_csv(ETS_output1, 'Submissions/ETS1.csv')
```
